issue.body:
## Goal

Replace the mocked claims domain with a state-canonical Postgres store, a transactional outbox, and an Inngest worker layer — realising [ADR-008](docs/adr/adr008-canonical-claim-store-transactional-outbox.md) (Accepted) and the read shape [ADR-007](docs/adr/adr007-append-only-claim-audit-history.md) assumes. Today the entire claims domain is a module-level `mockClaims` array behind tRPC (`src/server/api/mock.ts`); `schema.ts` holds only the better-auth tables. This epic delivers the outbox + worker scaffolding the real AI assessment agent and its observability will build on.

## Business Context

**Problem**: A claim write must fan out side effects (run an assessment, dispatch comms), but there is no durable seam between "the canonical claim committed" and "the side effect was scheduled." The domain is entirely mocked, so nothing persists and no worker layer exists.

**Opportunity**: A transactional outbox closes that gap with at-least-once delivery, and moving the assessment pass into a durable worker gives the future Anthropic-backed agent a retryable, observable seam to land in (the worker runs `mockAssess` logic for now — the seam is the deliverable).

**Success metrics**: All 5 canonical tables live; all 8 claims procedures (`list`, `byId` + 6 mutations) cut over to Postgres; both workers + both crons running on the local Inngest dev server; `mock.ts` deleted; `make check` / `make build` / `make test_app` green.

**Stakeholders**: Engineering (foundation for the production assessor, epic #19 / milestone M1.5); analysts (no UX change — the cutover is a data-source swap).

## Scope

**In Scope**:
- `outbox` table + typed outbox module (`buildOutboxEvent` / `sendPostCommit`).
- Canonical `claims` + `rounds` + `analyst_reviews` + `communications` tables; `claim_ref_seq` sequence-backed display key.
- Typed Inngest client, route handler, function registry, `EventSchemas` (Zod) event surface.
- Two workers — assessment (`mockAssess` logic) and comms-dispatch — plus two crons (sweep, prune).
- Cut over all reads and all 6 mutations from the mock to `db.batch` over Postgres.
- Typesafe seed fixtures; delete `mock.ts`.
- Local-dev Inngest dev-server wiring; env keys added to `src/env.js` as optional.

**Out of Scope** (explicitly):
- The real Anthropic-backed assessment agent (worker runs `mockAssess` logic; the seam is the deliverable).
- A rich persisted agent trace and any analytics / KPI-materialisation worker.
- Prod Inngest Cloud provisioning + Terraform secrets (design §9 follow-on; prod login is blocked on the better-auth callback issue regardless).
- Real PDS retrieval, real email provider (comms stays mocked — `sent_at` only).
- Any UI change — the cutover is a data-source swap, so the tRPC procedures keep returning the same `Claim`.
- Capturing the two ADR candidates (sync/async boundary; hourly-sweep amendment to ADR-008) — routed to a separate `/domain-model` run.

## Key Deliverables

Each is a vertical tracer-bullet slice and becomes a child issue:

1. **Outbox tracer** — `outbox` table + typed module + Inngest client/route/registry + sweep/prune skeleton, proven on one standalone event.
2. **Canonical claims schema + read cutover** — all 5 tables, `claim_ref_seq`, fixtures; `claims.list`/`byId` reconstruct from Postgres (writes still mock).
3. **Intake → assessment vertical** — `create` mutation `db.batch` + assessment worker (`mockAssess` logic), `request_id` fence, auto-decide → comms path.
4. **Analyst rail** — `agree`/`challenge`/`override` cut over to `db.batch`; delta-refactor of `claim-actions.ts`.
5. **Comms + QA** — comms-dispatch worker + `dispatchCommunication`/`qaReview` cutover; delete `mock.ts`.

## Timeline

**Target completion**: aligned to milestone **M1.5 — Production assessor build** (due 2026-07-01); this epic is the foundation seam the production assessor (#19) plugs into.

## Dependencies

- Children #1 (outbox tracer) and #2 (claims schema + reads) are independent and can start in parallel.
- The spine is linear thereafter: #3 needs #1 + #2; #4 needs #3; #5 needs #4.

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| `db.batch` over `neon-http` semantics misunderstood (single BEGIN…COMMIT, no statement-to-statement data flow) | High | All `id`s are JS-generated UUIDs so nothing in a batch threads data; tracer slice proves the pattern on one event before any claims write depends on it |
| Worker double-appends a round / double-sends a comm on retry | High | DB-enforced fences — `rounds.request_id` UNIQUE + `onConflictDoNothing`, `communications.sent_at IS NULL` guard, Inngest `concurrency:{key,limit:1}` |
| Post-commit `inngest.send` failure silently drops a side effect | Medium | Best-effort send is swallowed; hourly sweep re-delivers any `processed_at IS NULL` row (ADR-008 contract) |
| Mock/DB drift if both stores coexist | Medium | `mock.ts` deleted in the final slice once every read+write is cut over |

## References

- Design: `thoughts/designs/2026-06-22-transactional-outbox-inngest-foundation.md`
- ADR: `docs/adr/adr008-canonical-claim-store-transactional-outbox.md` (Accepted)
- ADR: `docs/adr/adr007-append-only-claim-audit-history.md` (Proposed)
- ADR: `docs/adr/adr006-shadow-aware-claims-ui.md` (Proposed)
- Reference implementation: sibling `rekurve` project (the hardened outbox pattern this consolidates)

labels.name: epic
labels.description: Parent epic tracking multiple child slices

milestone.title: M1.5 — Production assessor build
milestone.description: Epic #19: router + doctrine prompt v2 + Bedrock re-baseline (design 2026-06-11)
